introSection:
Do-Dodonpa (Japanese: ド・ドドンパ), previously Dodonpa, was an S&S Worldwide launched roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. It opened in 2001 and had the highest launch acceleration of any coaster in the world at 32.1 (3.27 g). It was also the fastest coaster in the world to feature an inversion, a tall vertical loop (formerly a top hat).

The control system was provided by Deterministic Systems, Inc.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Dodonpa was announced in June 2001. Upon opening on 21 December 2001, it was the fastest roller coaster in the world with a top speed of, which was reached in 1.8 seconds.

The roller coaster closed on 2 October 2016 for a major remodel. It reopened on 15 July 2017. This overhaul involved replacing the ride's top hat with a large vertical loop. The refurbishment also saw the ride's speed increased to and the ride's acceleration increased to 32.1  (3.27 g). The name was changed from Dodonpa to Do-Dodonpa.

On 12 August 2021, Do-Dodonpa was closed indefinitely for safety investigations following riders suffering fractured bones.

On 13 March 2024, Fuji-Q Highland announced that Do-Dodonpa would not be reopening and would be removed. As of early 2026, the ride's loop remains.

sections.title: Etymology
sections.content:
The name "Dodonpa" came from the notes used by taiko drummers. In the queue line, guests could hear these notes. At launch, they hear a voice in Japanese running through a checklist and counting down before the launch. The dialogue occasionally changes, and, to surprise the riders, there is often a "false" countdown and "failed" launch followed by an "accidental" launch.

The ride's name was changed to Do-Dodonpa when it was refurbished in 2017.

sections.title: Images
sections.content: Photographs of the roller coaster prior to its refurbishment in 2016.

fullText:
Do-Dodonpa (Japanese: ド・ドドンパ), previously Dodonpa, was an S&S Worldwide launched roller coaster located at Fuji-Q Highland in Fujiyoshida, Yamanashi, Japan. It opened in 2001 and had the highest launch acceleration of any coaster in the world at 32.1 (3.27 g). It was also the fastest coaster in the world to feature an inversion, a tall vertical loop (formerly a top hat).

The control system was provided by Deterministic Systems, Inc.

Dodonpa was announced in June 2001. Upon opening on 21 December 2001, it was the fastest roller coaster in the world with a top speed of, which was reached in 1.8 seconds.

The roller coaster closed on 2 October 2016 for a major remodel. It reopened on 15 July 2017. This overhaul involved replacing the ride's top hat with a large vertical loop. The refurbishment also saw the ride's speed increased to and the ride's acceleration increased to 32.1  (3.27 g). The name was changed from Dodonpa to Do-Dodonpa.

On 12 August 2021, Do-Dodonpa was closed indefinitely for safety investigations following riders suffering fractured bones.

On 13 March 2024, Fuji-Q Highland announced that Do-Dodonpa would not be reopening and would be removed. As of early 2026, the ride's loop remains.

The name "Dodonpa" came from the notes used by taiko drummers. In the queue line, guests could hear these notes. At launch, they hear a voice in Japanese running through a checklist and counting down before the launch. The dialogue occasionally changes, and, to surprise the riders, there is often a "false" countdown and "failed" launch followed by an "accidental" launch.

The ride's name was changed to Do-Dodonpa when it was refurbished in 2017.





Photographs of the roller coaster prior to its refurbishment in 2016.
